report_metadata.cancer_type: Radiation-Induced Undifferentiated Pleomorphic Sarcoma of the Neck
report_metadata.stage: 6 cm tumor in previously irradiated field (post-Hodgkin's RT 25 years ago)
molecular_profile: History of mediastinal/neck radiation for Hodgkin lymphoma

treatments.name: Wide surgical excision with free flap reconstruction

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple retrospective series demonstrate surgery is the primary treatment for radiation-induced sarcoma (RIS). R0/R1 resection correlates with significantly better DSS and OS vs R2 resection. No RCTs exist due to rarity.

survival_benefit.rationale: Surgery alone median OS ~42 months for RIS. 5-year OS 47% for radiation-associated UPS. R0 resection is the strongest predictor of favorable outcome. Head/neck location challenges wide margin achievement.

accessibility.rationale: Requires head and neck surgical oncology center with microvascular reconstruction capability. Free flap reconstruction (ALT, pectoralis major, fibula) often needed for large defects in fibrotic irradiated tissue.

safety_profile.rationale: Surgery in previously irradiated fibrotic tissue is technically challenging. Wound healing impaired. Higher complication rates: flap failure, wound dehiscence, fistula. Must assess for radiation-induced coronary disease before GA. Carotid artery involvement risk.

biomarker_match.rationale: Complex karyotype UPS does not have targeted therapy options. Surgery is histology-agnostic and remains the best local control approach regardless of molecular profile.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: En bloc surgical excision of the sarcoma with wide margins (ideally 2+ cm) in the irradiated field. Requires resection of surrounding fibrotic tissue to achieve negative margins. Free tissue transfer reconstruction (anterolateral thigh, pectoralis major, or other flaps) fills the surgical defect and brings vascularized tissue into the radiation-damaged field.

key_evidence.study_name: Survival and Margin Status in Head and Neck RIS and De Novo Sarcomas

notable_side_effects: Wound healing complications in irradiated tissue

treatments.name: Neoadjuvant doxorubicin + ifosfamide followed by surgery

evidence_level.rationale: Doxorubicin-based chemotherapy is the standard first-line for advanced STS. UPS has 42.3% response rate to doxorubicin/ifosfamide (vs 6.9% doxorubicin alone). Neoadjuvant approach may shrink tumor to enable better resection.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 7.4 months and OS 14.3 months with AI combination. In UPS specifically, AI showed improved OS vs doxorubicin alone (HR 0.44). Neoadjuvant use may convert borderline resectable to resectable.

accessibility.rationale: Doxorubicin and ifosfamide widely available at oncology centers. Standard regimen for advanced soft tissue sarcoma.

safety_profile.rationale: CRITICAL CONCERN: Doxorubicin cardiotoxicity in patient with likely radiation-induced coronary artery disease from prior Hodgkin's RT. Cumulative cardiac dose must be strictly monitored. Baseline cardiac evaluation mandatory. Ifosfamide causes hemorrhagic cystitis, neurotoxicity. Myelosuppression.

biomarker_match.rationale: UPS is one of the sarcoma subtypes most responsive to doxorubicin/ifosfamide. Complex karyotype does not predict resistance. However, radiation-induced sarcomas may have reduced chemotherapy penetration in fibrotic tissue.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Doxorubicin intercalates DNA and inhibits topoisomerase II, causing double-strand breaks. Ifosfamide is an alkylating agent that crosslinks DNA. Combined, they achieve higher response rates in UPS than doxorubicin alone. Neoadjuvant administration aims to shrink the 6cm tumor and facilitate surgical resection with negative margins.

treatments.name: Perioperative pembrolizumab + preoperative RT + surgery (SU2C-SARC032 regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: SU2C-SARC032 phase 2 trial demonstrated improved outcomes with perioperative pembrolizumab + preop RT + surgery for high-risk localized UPS. Landmark result establishing immunotherapy in sarcoma. 2024 data.

survival_benefit.rationale: SU2C-SARC032 showed improved outcomes for stage III UPS and pleomorphic/dedifferentiated liposarcoma with addition of pembrolizumab to standard preop RT + surgery. UPS has 23% ORR to pembrolizumab monotherapy (SARC-028).

accessibility.rationale: Pembrolizumab FDA approved but not specifically for sarcoma. SU2C-SARC032 data may lead to approval. Currently off-label use or clinical trial access.

safety_profile.rationale: MAJOR CONCERN: Reirradiation component in previously irradiated field. Prior Hodgkin RT 25 years ago limits reirradiation dose. Cumulative carotid dose <120 Gy required. irAEs from pembrolizumab. Surgery in fibrotic tissue.

biomarker_match.rationale: UPS is the most immunogenic STS subtype with highest response to checkpoint inhibitors. Complex karyotype may correlate with higher tumor mutational burden. PD-L1 expression in UPS correlates with response (75% of responders PD-L1+).

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab primes anti-tumor immunity. Preoperative radiation induces immunogenic cell death, releasing tumor neoantigens and priming immune response. Surgery removes bulk disease. Adjuvant pembrolizumab maintains immune surveillance. UPS is uniquely responsive among sarcomas due to high PD-L1 expression and tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes.

key_evidence.journal: ESMO Congress / Lancet Oncology (pending full publication)

treatments.name: Reirradiation with IMRT or proton therapy (adjuvant or neoadjuvant)

evidence_level.rationale: Reirradiation is feasible but limited data for sarcoma specifically. ASTRO and case series support reirradiation with careful dose constraints. Proton therapy offers dosimetric advantage in reirradiation.

survival_benefit.rationale: Adjuvant RT for head and neck sarcoma improves local control. However, reirradiation limited by prior dose (Hodgkin RT ~36-40 Gy, 25 years ago). Some tissue recovery expected over 25 years but fibrotic changes permanent.

accessibility.rationale: Reirradiation requires specialized planning with prior RT records. Proton therapy limited to ~40 centers. IMRT reirradiation more widely available but higher normal tissue doses.

safety_profile.rationale: HIGH RISK: Cumulative dose to carotid arteries must stay <120 Gy to prevent blowout. Osteoradionecrosis risk elevated. Mandible/cervical spine toxicity. Must avoid re-irradiating heart given existing radiation-induced coronary disease. Fibrotic tissue tolerates reirradiation poorly.

biomarker_match.rationale: UPS responds to radiation but radiation-induced sarcomas arise specifically because of radiation damage. Reirradiation carries theoretical concern of inducing further malignancy, though this is a minor consideration given current tumor threat.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Reirradiation delivers additional radiation to the sarcoma bed, either preoperatively to shrink tumor/sterilize margins or postoperatively for residual disease. Proton therapy's Bragg peak allows reduced dose to previously irradiated structures. IMRT with careful dose constraints limits cumulative organ-at-risk doses.

key_evidence.study_name: Re-Irradiation for Head and Neck Cancer: Cumulative Dose to OAR and Late Side Effects

treatments.name: Doxorubicin monotherapy (with cardiac monitoring)

evidence_level.rationale: Doxorubicin monotherapy is the standard first-line for advanced STS. Level 1 evidence from multiple phase 3 trials. ORR ~7% for UPS with monotherapy but median OS similar to combination (12.8 months).

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 12.8 months with doxorubicin alone. Lower ORR than combination (7% vs 42%) but similar OS. In radiation-induced sarcoma, chemotherapy less effective due to fibrotic tissue reducing drug penetration.

accessibility.rationale: Doxorubicin universally available. Standard first-line sarcoma therapy at all oncology centers.

safety_profile.rationale: CRITICAL CONCERN: Doxorubicin cardiotoxicity in patient with radiation-induced coronary disease from prior Hodgkin's RT. Lifetime cumulative dose limit 450 mg/m2. Requires baseline echocardiography, MUGA, and serial cardiac monitoring. Consider dexrazoxane cardioprotection. Myelosuppression.

biomarker_match.rationale: UPS moderately responsive to doxorubicin. Complex karyotype does not predict specific resistance pattern. Radiation-induced sarcomas may have impaired drug delivery.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Doxorubicin is an anthracycline that intercalates DNA and inhibits topoisomerase II, inducing double-strand DNA breaks and apoptosis. Cardiotoxicity is dose-dependent and cumulative, mediated by free radical generation and iron-mediated oxidative damage to cardiomyocytes.

treatments.name: Pazopanib (second-line or doxorubicin-intolerant)

evidence_level.rationale: PALETTE phase 3 trial established pazopanib for advanced STS after prior chemotherapy. JCOG1802 (2ND-STEP) 2024 showed pazopanib best PFS/OS among second-line options for non-liposarcoma STS. FDA approved.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 3.7 months, OS 15.7 months in JCOG1802. DCR 64.9%. Pazopanib preferred over trabectedin and eribulin for non-liposarcoma subtypes including UPS. PALETTE showed PFS 4.6 vs 1.6 months.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for advanced STS after prior chemotherapy. Oral medication. Widely available.

safety_profile.rationale: Oral TKI with manageable toxicity. Hypertension, diarrhea, fatigue, hand-foot syndrome. Less cardiotoxic than doxorubicin (important for this patient). Hepatotoxicity requires monitoring. No direct cardiac muscle toxicity.

biomarker_match.rationale: Pazopanib targets VEGFR, PDGFR, KIT. Non-specific mechanism. Better for non-liposarcoma STS subtypes including UPS per JCOG1802 subgroup analysis.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pazopanib is a multi-targeted tyrosine kinase inhibitor blocking VEGFR-1/2/3, PDGFR-alpha/beta, and c-KIT, inhibiting tumor angiogenesis and proliferation. Oral administration. Particularly effective in non-liposarcoma soft tissue sarcomas including UPS.

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab monotherapy (off-label for UPS)

evidence_level.rationale: SARC-028 phase 2 showed 23% ORR in UPS expansion cohort (2 CR, 7 PR in 40 patients). Alliance A091401 showed 16% ORR with nivolumab/ipilimumab. UPS is the most immunotherapy-responsive STS subtype.

survival_benefit.rationale: 23% ORR with some durable responses. 75% of responders were PD-L1 positive. Median PFS and OS data limited by phase 2 design. Some patients achieve prolonged remission.

accessibility.rationale: Pembrolizumab FDA approved for other indications but off-label for STS. May be accessible through MSI-H/TMB-H pathway if tumor qualifies. Complex karyotype may correlate with higher TMB.

safety_profile.rationale: Well tolerated compared to doxorubicin. No cardiotoxicity (critical advantage in this patient). irAEs manageable. Better safety profile for patient with radiation-induced coronary disease.

biomarker_match.rationale: UPS is the most immunogenic STS subtype. Complex karyotype may indicate higher TMB. PD-L1 expression predicts response (75% of responders). Should test PD-L1 and TMB.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody restoring T-cell anti-tumor immunity. UPS has the highest immune infiltration among STS subtypes, with significant PD-L1 expression. Complex karyotype tumors may have higher neoantigen burden driving immune recognition.

treatments.name: Sintilimab + doxorubicin + ifosfamide (immunochemotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 single-arm trial of sintilimab + AI in advanced UPS, SS, MLPS, and DDLPS. 2024 ASCO presentation. Early promising data. Not yet standard of care.

survival_benefit.rationale: Combination of checkpoint inhibitor with standard chemotherapy may improve responses over chemotherapy alone. Phase 2 data pending full publication. Theoretical synergy of immunogenic cell death from chemo + checkpoint blockade.

accessibility.rationale: Sintilimab not FDA approved in the US (approved in China). Would need clinical trial access or substitution with pembrolizumab + AI (off-label combination).

safety_profile.rationale: CRITICAL: Doxorubicin cardiotoxicity concern compounded by irAEs. Patient with radiation-induced coronary disease at high risk. irAEs may include myocarditis (rare but potentially fatal with cardiac comorbidity).

biomarker_match.rationale: UPS specifically included in trial. Immunogenic sarcoma subtype with potential for enhanced response to immunochemotherapy combination.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Anti-PD-1 antibody (sintilimab) combined with doxorubicin/ifosfamide chemotherapy. Chemotherapy-induced immunogenic cell death releases tumor neoantigens while checkpoint blockade prevents T-cell exhaustion, potentially enhancing anti-tumor immunity beyond either approach alone.
biomarker_requirements: UPS, SS, MLPS, or DDLPS histology

treatments.name: Durvalumab + pazopanib combination

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial of durvalumab + pazopanib in advanced STS published in Nature Communications 2024. Evaluating immune checkpoint + anti-angiogenic combination.

survival_benefit.rationale: Combination of anti-PD-L1 + anti-VEGFR aims to convert 'cold' tumors to 'hot' via anti-angiogenic immune modulation. Early-phase data. UPS may be more responsive than other STS subtypes.

accessibility.rationale: Clinical trial access. Neither durvalumab nor pazopanib approved for this specific combination in STS. Pazopanib individually FDA approved for STS.

safety_profile.rationale: No anthracycline cardiotoxicity (important advantage). Hypertension from pazopanib. irAEs from durvalumab. Hepatotoxicity monitoring needed. Better cardiac safety than doxorubicin-based regimens.

biomarker_match.rationale: Anti-angiogenic + immune checkpoint approach may benefit UPS with high vascularity and immune infiltration. Complex karyotype tumors may have higher neoantigen load.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Durvalumab (anti-PD-L1) blocks immune checkpoint while pazopanib (multi-TKI targeting VEGFR/PDGFR) inhibits tumor angiogenesis and normalizes tumor vasculature, improving immune cell infiltration. Anti-angiogenic therapy may convert immunologically 'cold' tumors to 'hot'.

treatments.name: Cardiac evaluation and coronary disease management (prerequisite)

evidence_level.rationale: ESC and AHA guidelines mandate cardiac evaluation in Hodgkin lymphoma survivors who received mediastinal RT. Radiation-induced coronary artery disease (RICAD) affects up to 25% of patients 10-25+ years after treatment. Well-established screening protocols.

survival_benefit.rationale: RICAD is the second most common cause of morbidity and mortality in Hodgkin lymphoma survivors after relapse. Undiagnosed CAD can cause perioperative MI or heart failure during anthracycline therapy. Must be managed before sarcoma treatment begins.

accessibility.rationale: Cardiac evaluation (echocardiography, stress testing, CT coronary angiography) widely available. Standard cardiology workup.

safety_profile.rationale: Diagnostic workup with minimal risk. Identification and treatment of coronary disease before sarcoma therapy prevents potentially fatal cardiac events during surgery or chemotherapy.

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient received Hodgkin's mediastinal/neck RT 25 years ago - peak RICAD risk period (2-3 decades). Silent CAD common due to autonomic dysfunction. Troponin, BNP, echocardiography needed.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Comprehensive cardiac evaluation including echocardiography (EF assessment), CT coronary angiography or stress testing, and biomarkers (troponin, BNP) to detect radiation-induced coronary artery disease, cardiomyopathy, valvulopathy, or pericardial disease. Results guide oncologic treatment selection: cardiac disease limits anthracycline use and requires perioperative optimization.

treatments.name: Gemcitabine + docetaxel (cardio-sparing alternative)

evidence_level.rationale: Gemcitabine/docetaxel is an established second-line or alternative first-line regimen for STS. GeDDiS trial showed similar OS to doxorubicin monotherapy. Less cardiotoxic than anthracyclines.

survival_benefit.rationale: Similar OS to doxorubicin in GeDDiS trial. Lower ORR. Primarily used when anthracyclines contraindicated. Radiation-induced sarcomas may have impaired drug penetration.

accessibility.rationale: Gemcitabine and docetaxel widely available. Standard combination at all oncology centers.

safety_profile.rationale: NO cardiotoxicity (major advantage for this patient). Myelosuppression (especially neutropenia with docetaxel). Pulmonary toxicity rare. Peripheral neuropathy from docetaxel. Fluid retention.

biomarker_match.rationale: Non-specific mechanism. UPS responds modestly. Main advantage is cardiac safety in a patient who cannot safely receive anthracyclines.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Gemcitabine is a nucleoside analog that inhibits DNA synthesis. Docetaxel stabilizes microtubules, preventing cell division. Combination provides non-anthracycline cytotoxic option for patients with cardiac contraindications to doxorubicin.

treatments.name: Trabectedin (second-line)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for unresectable/metastatic liposarcoma or leiomyosarcoma after prior anthracycline. JCOG1802 showed median PFS 2.9 months, OS 14.8 months. Less effective in UPS than LPS/LMS.

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 2.9 months, OS 14.8 months in JCOG1802 (all STS). DCR 50%. Less preferred than pazopanib for non-liposarcoma subtypes including UPS.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for STS. Requires central venous access for 24-hour infusion. Available at oncology centers.

safety_profile.rationale: Hepatotoxicity (ALT/AST elevation), neutropenia, nausea. No significant cardiotoxicity (advantage). Requires hepatic function monitoring.

biomarker_match.rationale: Most effective in translocation-related sarcomas (liposarcoma, leiomyosarcoma). UPS with complex karyotype is not the ideal histology for trabectedin.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Trabectedin binds the minor groove of DNA, interfering with transcription factors and DNA repair mechanisms. Also modulates tumor microenvironment by depleting tumor-associated macrophages. Most effective in liposarcoma and leiomyosarcoma.

clinical_trials.title: Perioperative Pembrolizumab + Preoperative RT + Surgery for High-Risk Localized UPS/Pleomorphic LPS
clinical_trials.early_results: Improved outcomes with addition of pembrolizumab to preop RT + surgery for stage III UPS. Establishes immunotherapy in sarcoma paradigm.

clinical_trials.title: 2ND-STEP: Randomized Phase 2 Comparing Trabectedin, Eribulin, and Pazopanib as Second-Line STS Treatment
clinical_trials.early_results: Pazopanib showed best PFS (3.7 months), OS (15.7 months), and DCR (64.9%) for non-liposarcoma STS.

clinical_trials.title: Sintilimab + Doxorubicin/Ifosfamide First-Line in Advanced UPS, SS, MLPS, DDLPS
biomarker_requirements: UPS, SS, MLPS, or DDLPS histology
clinical_trials.early_results: Presented at ASCO 2024. Results pending full publication.

clinical_trials.title: Durvalumab + Pazopanib in Advanced Soft Tissue Sarcoma
clinical_trials.early_results: Published Nature Communications 2024. Evaluating anti-PD-L1 + anti-angiogenic combination.

clinical_trials.title: Pembrolizumab in Advanced Soft Tissue Sarcoma (UPS Expansion)
clinical_trials.early_results: 23% ORR (2 CR, 7 PR in 40 patients). 75% of responders PD-L1+.

combination_strategies.rationale: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy may shrink the 6cm tumor to facilitate negative-margin resection in fibrotic irradiated tissue. UPS is the most chemo-responsive STS subtype. Must confirm adequate cardiac function first.
SU2C-SARC032 established perioperative pembrolizumab + preop RT + surgery for localized UPS. Reirradiation dose may need reduction given prior Hodgkin RT. UPS responds to immunotherapy (23% ORR).

Anti-angiogenic therapy may normalize tumor vasculature and enhance immune cell infiltration. No cardiac toxicity from either agent. Case reports of sustained responses to pazopanib + pembrolizumab in UPS.
If doxorubicin is deemed necessary, dexrazoxane (iron chelator) reduces anthracycline cardiotoxicity by ~50%. Allows cautious anthracycline use in patients with borderline cardiac function.
Non-anthracycline chemotherapy avoids cardiac risk. Immunogenic cell death from chemo may synergize with checkpoint blockade. UPS is immunotherapy-responsive. Preserves cardiac function.

supportive_care.approach: Comprehensive cardiac evaluation (echocardiography, CT coronary angiography, stress testing)
supportive_care.evidence: ESC/AHA guidelines mandate cardiac screening in Hodgkin survivors 10+ years post-mediastinal RT. RICAD affects 25% at 25+ years. Silent CAD common due to autonomic dysfunction.
supportive_care.benefit: Detects radiation-induced coronary artery disease, cardiomyopathy, valvulopathy before oncologic treatment. Guides treatment selection (anthracycline eligibility, surgical fitness).

supportive_care.evidence: Expert consensus recommends cardio-oncology co-management when anthracycline therapy is considered in patients with prior mediastinal RT or known cardiac disease
supportive_care.benefit: Optimizes cardiac medications (statins, ACE-I, beta-blockers), monitors cardiac function during treatment, manages dexrazoxane cardioprotection if needed

supportive_care.approach: Wound care specialist involvement for irradiated tissue surgery
supportive_care.evidence: Surgical wounds in previously irradiated tissue have significantly higher complication rates. Free flap reconstruction brings vascularized tissue to improve healing.
supportive_care.benefit: Reduces wound dehiscence, prevents flap failure, manages chronic radiation-induced skin changes

supportive_care.evidence: Neck extensor weakness and cervicothoracic pain are common in Hodgkin lymphoma survivors after neck RT. Post-surgical rehabilitation critical for functional recovery.
supportive_care.benefit: Addresses pre-existing radiation fibrosis, improves range of motion, supports recovery from neck surgery

supportive_care.approach: Survivorship monitoring for additional late effects of Hodgkin's RT
supportive_care.evidence: Hodgkin survivors face elevated risk of thyroid dysfunction, secondary cancers, pulmonary fibrosis, and carotid stenosis in addition to cardiac disease
supportive_care.benefit: Comprehensive screening detects other radiation late effects that may complicate sarcoma treatment
